expectations.text: Names code-simplifier as a dedicated subagent launched after implementation to find refactoring opportunities.
expectations.evidence: No code-simplifier agent is mentioned anywhere in the response. Simplicity work is assigned to a `code-reviewer` agent with a simplicity focus, not a dedicated code-simplifier subagent.

expectations.text: Names one or more code-reviewer subagents launched for quality review, each with a stated focus (e.g. simplicity, correctness, conventions).
expectations.evidence: Three `code-reviewer` agents are launched in parallel with clearly stated focuses: Simplicity, Correctness, and Conventions — each with a concrete focus description.

expectations.text: Places the code-simplifier pass before or alongside the code-reviewer pass, not after it.
expectations.evidence: No code-simplifier pass exists at all. The simplicity concern is folded into a code-reviewer agent launched in parallel with the other reviewers, not a separate prior step.

expectations.text: Gives each subagent a concrete focus description, not just a generic 'review the code' instruction.
expectations.evidence: Each of the three code-reviewer agents receives a multi-sentence prompt listing specific things to look for (e.g., cache key generation, race conditions, import patterns, naming conventions).

expectations.text: Does not treat refactoring and quality review as a single unified step performed by one agent.
expectations.evidence: Three separate agents handle three distinct concerns. No single agent combines all review types. Simplicity, correctness, and conventions are split across independent agents.
